replication_study.hypothesis: Participants who value the prospect when it is framed as a risky "lottery ticket" will report a lower mean willingness-to-pay (WTP) than participants who value the identical prospect framed as a less-risky "gift certificate" (two-tailed test of mean difference).

data_plan.source_type: Online behavioural experiment (Qualtrics survey distributed to MTurk workers)
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Single independent replication run (not part of original four experiments)
data_plan.sample_size: 281 usable rows before exclusions; final analytic N expected ≈200 after comprehension & value caps (see Script.R)

qualification.explanation: The dataset reproduces the exact framing manipulation and WTP measure while coming from a fresh data collection, allowing an independent robustness check.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Contains identical operational variables (Lot_WTP/Gift_WTP, frame checks) noted in Script.R codebook, matching descriptions in post_registration.json (frame label, WTP buyer task).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Collected in 2020, not 2013; only two frames (lottery vs gift) vs multiple frames in original Exp.3-4; sample size (≈281) differs from each original experiment (119–605) (post_registration.json).

data_plan.notes: Some WTP entries include non-numeric strings (e.g., "$20", text). Script.R already recodes to numeric (Lot_WTPc/Gift_WTPc). The script filters values >20 USD in a robustness check.

planned_method.steps:
1. Load CSV into R.
2. Recreate condition variable (Cond: lottery=0, gift=1) using comprehension-check logic.
3. Create numeric WTP dependent variable based on condition (Lot_WTPc or Gift_WTPc).
4. Exclude rows with missing WTP or failed comprehension checks (check ≠ 3).
5. Primary analysis: independent-samples t-test of WTP by Cond.
6. Report group means, Cohen's d, 95% CI, p-value.
7. Robustness: repeat t-test on subset WTP ≤ 20 USD.
planned_method.models: Independent-samples t-test; effect size Cohen’s d; descriptive statistics.
planned_method.control_variables: None (random assignment); comprehension check used for exclusion not covariate.
planned_method.tools_software: R (>=4.0) with packages psych, yarrr (for Cohen's d), tidyverse (for data handling).

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Mean difference in WTP (gift − lottery) and Cohen's d

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion of rows with NA in key variables or failing comprehension check.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Only one focal test; no correction needed.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Statistical significance at α = .05 (two-tailed). Directional consistency with hypothesis (gift > lottery) required for successful replication.

files.Script.R: End-to-end analysis script: loads data, creates variables, filters comprehension check, performs t-tests and computes Cohen's d. Saves filtered data (dataS.csv) for transparency.
files.Data_Cleaned_22102020.csv: Cleaned participant-level dataset with original Qualtrics metadata removed. Contains recoded numeric WTP variables (Lot_WTPc, Gift_WTPc) and comprehension checks.
files..DS_Store: MacOS metadata file – can be ignored or deleted.

codebase.notes: Script assumes working directory; we will modify execution command to run inside container with /app/data mounted. Dependencies limited to CRAN packages psych and yarrr.

analysis.instructions: Inside the container, set working directory to /app/data/original/32/input/replication_data and run Rscript Script.R. Results (group means, t-test, Cohen's d) will print to console and dataS.csv will be written to the same folder.
analysis.comparison_metrics: 1. Direction of mean difference (gift > lottery). 2. Magnitude: mean difference and Cohen's d vs original (~d ≈ 0.66 from η²=.11). 3. p-value < .05.
